You are gathering ground-truth facts about the repository at C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend so another agent can write its canonical AGENTS.md. Do NOT write or edit any files. Inspect actual files (package.json, angular.json, Dockerfile, cloudbuild*.yaml, .github/workflows, src/ structure, .env examples, existing docs/, ignore files). Be thorough ("very thorough").

Return a structured plain-text report with these labeled sections, citing real file paths. Where something is unknown, say "UNKNOWN" and how to verify it. NEVER include secret values.

1. PROJECT SUMMARY: what this app is (framework, version), who uses it, key moving parts. Derive from package.json, README.md, angular.json.
2. REPO STRUCTURE: list top-level dirs and the important src/ subdirectories. Classify each as: project-owned code, generated code, third-party/vendor/framework, build artifacts, docs, scripts, deployment. Note the Vex template (vendor) vs custom code boundary if discernible.
3. CUSTOM-CODE BOUNDARY: where does project-owned custom code live vs vendored/demo template code? Look at AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md hints and src/app/pages structure.
4. BUILD/RUN/TEST/LINT: exact commands from package.json scripts (yarn/npm). Node/Angular versions. Test runner (jest?). Lint config.
5. DEPLOYMENT: Dockerfile contents summary, cloudbuild yaml (build steps, image names, tags), any .github/workflows (names, triggers), deploy platform (Cloud Run / Coolify / other), trigger method, rollback, where runtime env vars live, whether SSH is used. Extract real image names, project IDs, service names, tags if present.
6. ENV VARS / CONFIG: every env var referenced in code/config/env example files/deployment. Variable name + purpose + where stored. NO values. Config files (environment.ts, proxy.conf, etc.).
7. DATA MODEL / EXTERNAL IDENTIFIERS: any UUIDs, project IDs, container names, external-system IDs, deployment targets, webhook names found in config/docs/code.
8. CONTAINERS/SERVICES: any runtime services/containers (from Dockerfile/compose/cloudbuild).
9. EXISTING DOCS INVENTORY: list every .md file (root + docs/ + any folder-level READMEs). For each, one line on what it covers and whether it looks stale/redundant/vague/misleading vs current code. Read AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, HANDOFF.md, README.md, and each docs/*.md.
10. IGNORE FILES: contents of .claudeignore and .cursorignore; whether .copilotignore exists; whether they match.
11. INCIDENTS/QUIRKS/PENDING: any documented critical incidents, intentional quirks, or pending work already recorded in the existing docs (quote the headings). Any obvious stale pending items.
12. HANDOFF: summarize current HANDOFF.md content and whether the work it describes appears finished.

Keep it factual and high-signal. Quote exact commands, paths, image names, and identifiers.

content.content:
1	# Designflow Frontend
2	
3	Angular 21 SPA for the Designflow PLM platform. Covers the full product lifecycle: RFQ (vendor quoting and margin calculation), Item Library, Standardized Products, Art Piece management, and Production/Licensing Tracking.
4	
5	**Before working on this codebase, read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md).**
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Branch workflow
10	
11	Work only on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding code, sync GitHub `develop` into GitHub `sandbox-albert`, pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally, then make changes. Push commits to `sandbox-albert` and keep the PR from `sandbox-albert` to `develop` updated or create it if it is missing. Never merge to `main`.
12	
13	Add or update unit tests for new behavior before committing.
14	
15	---
16	
17	## Quick start
18	
19	```bash
20	# Install (uses yarn lockfile — do not use npm install)
21	yarn install
22	
23	# Serve locally (dev environment, port 4200)
24	npm run start
25	
26	# Run tests
27	npm run test
28	
29	# Lint
30	npm run lint
31	```
32	
33	## Builds
34	
35	| Command | Environment | API target |
36	|---|---|---|
37	| `npm run build:production` | production | `api.designflow.app` |
38	| `npm run build:develop` | staging | `api.sandbox.designflow.app` |
39	| `npm run build:sandbox-albert` | sandbox (Albert) | `api.alsand.designflow.app` |
40	
41	## Key directories
42	
43	| Path | Contents |
44	|---|---|
45	| `src/app/pages/` | Feature modules (RFQ, item library, tracking, admin) |
46	| `src/app/helpers/` | API service, models, interceptors, AG Grid column configs |
47	| `src/environments/` | Per-environment API base URLs |
48	| `docs/` | Architecture, development, configuration, deployment guides |
49	
50	## Documentation
51	
52	Start with [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md). The focused guides live in [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md), [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md), [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md), [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md), and [`docs/api-consumption.md`](docs/api-consumption.md).
53	
54	## Stack
55	
56	- **Angular 21.1.3** with Angular Material and CDK
57	- **AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1** — primary data grid for all feature modules
58	- **RxJS 7.8.1** — all async operations use `firstValueFrom()` (not `.toPromise()`)
59	- **PrimeNG 19.1.4** — supplementary UI components
60	- **Tailwind CSS 2.0.4**
61	- **@auth0/angular-jwt** — JWT token handling
62	- **@azure/msal-browser 5.x** — Microsoft SSO (Azure AD popup login)
63	- **nginx-brotli** — production serving with brotli compression
64	- **Google Cloud Run** — deployment target

1	# ==========================
2	# Stage 1: Build Angular App
3	# ==========================
4	FROM node:lts AS builder
5	
6	# Set build-time environment variables
7	ARG NODE_ENV
8	ENV NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV}
9	ARG COMMIT_SHA=local
10	
11	# Set working directory
12	WORKDIR /app
13	
14	# Activate Yarn 4 (Berry) — lockfile is in v4 format; node:lts ships corepack
15	RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare yarn@4.13.0 --activate
16	
17	# Install dependencies (leverage caching for faster builds)
18	COPY package.json yarn.lock .yarnrc.yml ./
19	RUN yarn install --immutable
20	
21	# Copy source code, run tests, then build Angular app
22	COPY . .
23	RUN TS=$(node -e " \
24	      const d=new Date(); \
25	      const date=d.toLocaleDateString('en-CA',{timeZone:'America/New_York'}); \
26	      const time=d.toLocaleTimeString('en-US',{timeZone:'America/New_York',hour:'numeric',minute:'2-digit',hour12:true,timeZoneName:'short'}); \
27	      console.log(date+' '+time);") && \
28	    printf 'export const APP_VERSION = {\n  commitHash: '"'"'%s'"'"',\n  pushedAt: '"'"'%s'"'"',\n};\n' \
29	    "$(echo "${COMMIT_SHA}" | cut -c1-7)" "$TS" > src/app/version.ts
30	RUN yarn test
31	RUN yarn build:${NODE_ENV}
32	
33	
34	# ===============================
35	# Stage 2: Serve App with NGINX
36	# ===============================
37	FROM macbre/nginx-brotli:1.19.10
38	
39	# Copy custom NGINX configuration
40	COPY --from=builder /app/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
41	
42	# Copy built Angular app to NGINX html directory
43	COPY --from=builder /app/dist/browser /usr/share/nginx/html
44	
45	# Expose HTTP port
46	EXPOSE 80
47	
48	# Default command provided by the base NGINX image
49

7	## 1. Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (2026-06-05)
8	
9	A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-frontend` and `designflow-backend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch `sandbox-albert` in both repos.
10	
11	### What it does
12	
13	Factory/vendor users see an **Upload Offers** button (`@if (role === 'vendor')`) in the RFQ toolbar. Clicking it opens a 3-stage dialog:
14	
15	1. **Form** — download a pre-formatted `.xlsx` template (pre-filled with factory name, date, Customer dropdown, Price Terms dropdown, 50 tall data rows with yellow image cells); name the RFQ group; drop-zone to select the completed file
16	2. **Uploading** — spinner while the API processes the file
17	3. **Result** — list of created items (price code + description) and any row-level errors (bilingual English · Chinese)
18	
19	On the backend, the import creates:
20	- One `RFQGroup` (named by the factory)
21	- One `RFQItem` per non-empty data row — price code `foff-XXXX` (global sequential; padded to 4 digits)
22	- One `RFQVendor` quote record per row (linked to the uploading factory's `factory_id` from JWT; `req_status = 2` = quoted; lands at @factories step)
23	- Embedded product images extracted from the xlsx and uploaded to DigitalOcean Spaces via `uploadBufferToSpaces`

76	## 1. Notification trigger points (backend — designflow-backend)
77	
78	Nothing fires `createNotification()` on business events. The notification system exists
79	(routes, service, frontend bell/badge) but no triggers have been wired.
80	
81	**Pattern to follow** (from the existing @mention handler):
82	
83	```js
84	setImmediate(async () => {
85	  try {
86	    await NotificationService.createManyNotifications(targets, {
87	      type: 'task_assignment',  // or 'status_change', 'approval'
88	      event: 'assigned',
89	      title: 'Task assigned',
90	      message: `${authorName} assigned a task to you`,
91	    });
92	  } catch {}
93	});
94	```
95	
96	**Where to wire it:**
97	- Task assignment: `services/task.service.js` or wherever `createTask` / `assignTask` lives
98	- RFQ status change: `routes/lib.router.js` PATCH handler for `rfq_status`
99	- Licensing approval: `routes/lib.router.js` PATCH handler for `licensing_status`

106	| `approval` | check_circle |
107	| anything else | notifications |

112	
113	Add a `/api/admin/hts-rates/search?desc={text}` endpoint that does a simple keyword match
114	over description fields in `data/hts-rates.json`. Inject the top 5 matches into the AI's
115	initial message as "Potentially relevant HTS headings." Zero new infrastructure — uses the
116	already-bundled USITC JSON file.
117	
118	**Why it matters:** The system prompt covers 9 chapters. For products outside those chapters,
119	the model falls back on training data. A simple keyword search would inject the exact USITC
120	subheadings the model should consider, improving accuracy on edge-case products.
121	
122	**Implementation sketch:**
123	- Backend: `helpers/hts-rates.js` — add `searchByDescription(text, limit = 5)` that filters
124	  `hts-rates.json` entries whose `description` field contains any token from `text`.
125	- Route: `routes/admin.router.js` — `GET /api/admin/hts-rates/search?desc={text}`
126	- Frontend: `ai-settings.service.ts` — add `searchHtsRates(desc: string)` method
127	- Inject in `initAiSession()` alongside CROSS results (non-fatal if empty or missing)

4	
5	Designflow Frontend is a pure Angular SPA — no server-side rendering. It communicates with a separate backend REST API over HTTPS. The frontend is responsible for all UI, routing, role enforcement, and business-logic calculations (margin chains, landed cost). The backend handles persistence and data retrieval.
6	
7	```
8	Browser
9	  └── Angular SPA (this repo)
10	        ├── AG Grid Enterprise (primary data display and editing)
11	        ├── Angular Material (forms, dialogs, snackbars)
12	        ├── Auth (JWT in localStorage, idle timeout)
13	        └── HTTP client
14	              └── Backend REST API (separate service)
15	                    ├── /api/core        ← main CRUD
16	                    ├── /api/tracking    ← production/licensing tracking
17	                    ├── /api/item_master ← item library
18	                    └── /api/data_sync   ← sync operations
19	```
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Module structure
24	
25	```
26	src/app/
27	├── app.module.ts              Root NgModule (providers, interceptors, AG Grid registration)
28	├── app-routing.module.ts      All route definitions and guards
29	├── app.component.ts           Root component: nav menu, query-param theme switching
30	├── custom-layout/             Shell layout wrapping all authenticated routes
31	├── pages/                     Feature modules (see below)
32	├── helpers/
33	│   ├── services/              API services (main.service.ts is the primary one)
34	│   ├── models/                TypeScript interfaces
35	│   ├── interceptor/           HTTP interceptors (auth, loader, error)
36	│   └── ag-grid/               AG Grid column definitions per feature
37	└── components/                Shared reusable components (global loader, file upload, etc.)
38	```
39	
40	---
41	
42	## Feature modules
43	
44	### RFQ (`src/app/pages/rfq/`)
45	
46	The primary feature module. Manages vendor quotes for product sourcing.
47	
48	**Key components:**
49	- `rfq.component.ts` — master grid, column wiring, valueSetter orchestration (~3600 lines)
50	- `detail-cell-renderer/` — detail row renderer for vendor quotes (AG Grid master-detail)
51	- `helpers/equation.ts` — all margin and sell-price calculations
52	- `helpers/api-error.ts` — shared HTTP error message extraction
53	
54	**Column configuration:** `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`
55	
56	**Dialog sub-components:** `addNewProduct/`, `duplicate_dialog/`, `container_cost_dialog/`, `duty_rate_dialog/`, `multi_quote/`
57	
58	See [`src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`](../src/app/pages/rfq/README.md) for the RFQ calculation pipeline and AG Grid patterns.
59	
60	### Item Library (`src/app/pages/itemLibrary/`)
61	
62	Product master data management. Single AG Grid with custom column definitions from `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/item.library.config.ts`.
63	
64	### Production Tracking (`src/app/pages/prod_tracking/`)
65	
66	Tracks production stages per item. Has its own `equation.ts` for production-specific calculations. Uses master-detail AG Grid (same pattern as RFQ).
67	
68	### Licensing Tracking (`src/app/pages/licensing_tracking/`)
69	
70	Tracks licensing items and royalty calculations. Has its own `equation.ts`.
71	
72	### Sample Tracking (`src/app/pages/sample_tracking/`)
73	
74	Tracks physical product samples moving between factories and the Ningbo/NYC offices
75	(request / factory-offer / USA-bought origins; bidirectional check-in/out). A reskin over the
76	`designflow-tracking` `/api/tracking/sample` API using the same building blocks as the other
77	tracking grids: master grid with a master-detail event timeline, plus dialogs for create,
78	check-in/out, sample detail (Info/Photos/Comments/Timeline), and box/group management. Photos
79	upload to DigitalOcean Spaces via a presigned-URL flow (`fetch` PUT bypasses the HTTP
80	interceptors, matching the existing pattern in `helper.service.ts`). The status state machine is
81	mirrored client-side in `helpers/sample.status.ts` for labels and next-status preview, but the
82	server is authoritative (`POST /sample/events`). Samples live in their own backend tables (not
83	`itemHeader`) and link back to licensing/production via `item_id_fk` / `prod_order_no_fk`.
84	User-facing labels avoid raw "inbound/outbound": a sample's direction shows as a **Journey**
85	(`Factory → NYC` / `NYC → Factory`) and boxes are created/shown by **route**
86	(`Ningbo → NYC` / `NYC → Ningbo`); the grid has a **Box** column (from the list endpoint's
87	derived `box_label`). To box samples: tick rows in the grid → **Boxes & Groups** → **Add selected**.
88	
89	### Standardized Products (`src/app/pages/standardized/`)
90	
91	Manages standardized/baseline product definitions used as templates for new RFQ items.
92	
93	### Admin / Editor (`src/app/pages/editor/`)
94	
95	Role-restricted to `admin`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`. Provides grid-level configuration editing.
96

1	# Deployment
2	
3	## Pipeline overview
4	
5	```
6	git push → Cloud Build trigger
7	  → Step 1: docker build (Node → yarn test → yarn build:<env> → nginx image)
8	  → Step 2: docker push → Artifact Registry
9	  → Step 3: gcloud run deploy → Cloud Run
10	```
11	
12	There is no GitHub Actions workflow. CI/CD is handled entirely by Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`).
13	
14	## Triggering a build
15	
16	Builds are triggered automatically by Cloud Build triggers configured in the Google Cloud Console. The trigger watches a specific branch and fires on push.
17	
18	- `sandbox-albert` branch → sandbox Cloud Build trigger → `_APP_ENV=sandbox-albert` → `build:sandbox-albert`
19	- `develop` branch → develop trigger → `_APP_ENV=develop` → `build:develop`
20	- `main` / `master` branch → production trigger → `_APP_ENV=production` → `build:production`
21	
22	Each trigger has its own substitution variables set in the Cloud Console (not in the YAML file).
23	
24	## Docker image
25	
26	**Build stages:**
27	
28	1. **Builder** (`node:lts`):
29	   - `WORKDIR /app`
30	   - `yarn install --frozen-lockfile --production=false`
31	   - `yarn test` — **tests must pass or the build fails**
32	   - `yarn build:${NODE_ENV}` — Angular build with environment substitution
33	   - Output: `dist/browser/`
34	
35	2. **Runtime** (`macbre/nginx-brotli:1.19.10`):
36	   - Copies `nginx.conf`
37	   - Copies `dist/browser/` to `/usr/share/nginx/html/`
38	   - Exposes port 80
39	   - Brotli-enabled nginx serves the SPA
40	
41	**Image tag format:**
42	```
43	{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT_ID}/{ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/{APP_ENV}/{APP_NAME}:{BUILD_ID}-{COMMIT_SHA}
44	```
45	
46	Example:
47	```
48	us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/designflow/production/designflow-frontend:abc123-d4e5f6a7
49	```
50	
51	## Cloud Run service
52	
53	The image is deployed to Google Cloud Run (managed platform, not GKE). Cloud Run scales to zero when idle. Configuration:
54	
55	| Parameter | Controlled by | Default (from cloudbuild.yaml) |
56	|---|---|---|
57	| CPU | `_CPU` trigger variable | `1` |
58	| Memory | `_MEMORY` trigger variable | `512Mi` |
59	| Concurrency | `_CONCURRENCY` trigger variable | `80` |
60	| Max instances | `_MAX_INSTANCES` trigger variable | `10` |
61	| Auth | `--allow-unauthenticated` | Public (no Cloud IAM auth) |
62	
63	Runtime environment variables are not injected into the frontend container — all config is baked in at build time.
64	
65	## Environments
66	
67	| Environment | Branch | API target | Purpose |
68	|---|---|---|---|
69	| `sandbox-albert` | `sandbox-albert` | `api.alsand.designflow.app` | Albert's personal dev/test environment |
70	| `staging` / `develop` | `develop` | `api.sandbox.designflow.app` | Shared staging environment |
71	| `production` | main branch | `api.designflow.app` | Live production |

What this project is 8 9 Designflow is a product-lifecycle-management (PLM) web application used by sourcing, merchandising, licensing, and production teams to manage the full journey of a product from concept to retail. The core workflows are: **RFQ** (request for quote — collect vendor pricing and compute landed-cost and margin chains), **Item Library** (product master data), **Standardized Products**, **Art Piece** management, and **Production/Licensing Tracking**. This repository is the Angular frontend only; the backend REST API is a separate service at `api.designflow.app`. The business-critical outcome is correct margin and landed-cost calculations in the RFQ grid — errors there directly affect buy prices and retailer quotes. 10 11 --- 12 13 ## 2. # Development Guide

## Prerequisites

- **Node.js LTS** (the version used by the Dockerfile builder stage)
- **Yarn** (the project uses a `yarn.lock` — do not use `npm install`)
- A running backend API (see backend repo) — or point `environment.ts` at `api.sandbox.designflow.app` to use the shared sandbox backend

## Install

```bash
yarn install
```

Do not use `npm install`. The repo uses a frozen Yarn lockfile. Running `npm install` will generate a `package-lock.json` that conflicts with `yarn.lock`.

## Serve locally

```bash
npm run start
```

This serves against `environment.ts` (localhost:5004 API by default). The dev server runs on `http://localhost:4200` with live reload. The `--max-old-space-size=8192` flag is set in the script — the build is memory-heavy.

To serve against the sandbox backend instead of a local one, temporarily change `environment.ts` to use the `environment.sandbox.ts` URLs. Revert before committing.

## Build locally

```bash
# Against production API
npm run build:production

# Against sandbox API (Albert's environment)
npm run build:sandbox-albert
```

Output lands in `dist/browser/`. The production build uses output hashing (cache-busting filenames).

On Windows PowerShell, the package scripts' Unix-style `NODE_ENV=...` prefix is not parsed. Use the equivalent command directly:

```powershell
$env:NODE_ENV='sandbox-albert'
node --max_old_space_size=8192 ./node_modules/@angular/cli/bin/ng build --output-hashing=all --configuration sandbox
```

## Run tests

```bash
yarn test                                        # full suite (~12 s)
yarn test --testPathPatterns="rfq.grid.config"   # single file

# Designflow Frontend API Consumption

The frontend is not an API provider. It is an Angular SPA that consumes the
Designflow API through the BFF.

## Environment Base URLs

| Environment file | Core | Item master | Tracking | Data sync |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `environment.ts` | `http://localhost:5004/api/core` | `http://localhost:5004/api/item_master` | `http://localhost:5004/api/tracking` | `http://localhost:5004/api/data_sync` |
| `environment.sandbox.ts` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
| `environment.staging.ts` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |
| `environment.prod.ts` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/core` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/item_master` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/tracking` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/data_sync` |

## Main API Service Consumers

| Frontend service | Backend base | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `main.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Core RFQ, vendors, grid layout, files, comments, notifications |
| `std.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Standardized product endpoints |
| `admin.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Admin reference data |
| `ai-settings.service.ts` | `environment.url` | AI model settings, tariffs, factory country map |
| `cross.service.ts` | `environment.url` | CBP CROSS search |
| `customer.service.ts` | `environment.url` | Customer CRUD and customer logo upload |
| `item_library.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /lib` | Item library |
| `item_detail.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /detail` | Item detail |
| `art_piece.service.ts` | `environment.item_master + /artPiece` | Art pieces |
| `tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking` | Production and sample tracking |
| `licensing_tracking.service.ts` | `environment.tracking + /lic` | Licensing tracking |
| `sync.service.ts` | `environment.data_sync` | Data sync calls |

## Auth

Frontend services attach the user JWT in `Authorization`. The BFF preserves that
user token as `X-User-Authorization` while it sends its Cloud Run OIDC token to
backend services.

   466 src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts
  4532 total

1	# RFQ Module
2	
3	This is the primary feature module. It manages vendor requests-for-quote and computes the full cost chain from FOB factory price to retail sell price.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## File map
8	
9	| File | Role |
10	|---|---|
11	| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~3600 lines. |
12	| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports — pure business math. |
13	| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
14	| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
15	| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
16	| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
17	| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
18	| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
19	| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
20	| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
21	| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
22	| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
23	| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
24	| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
25	| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
26	| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
27	| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
28	| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
29	| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |
30	
31	Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder.
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)`
36	
37	Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`.
38	
39	- `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`)
40	- `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)
41	
42	**Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11.
43	
44	In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`.
45	
46	---
47	
48	## Calculation pipeline
49	
50	**For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.**
51	
52	### Recalculation trigger chain
53	
54	When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls:
55	
56	```
57	calculateLandedCost()        ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed
58	recalculate_Lic_Gen()
59	  → setGenFobMargin          → gen_fob_sell_price
60	  → setGenMddpMargin         → gen_mddp_sell_price
61	  → setGenPoeMargin          → gen_poe_sell_price
62	  → setGenWhseMargin         → gen_whse_sell_price
63	  → setLicFobMargin          → lic_fob_sell_price
64	  → setLicMddpMargin         → lic_mddp_sell_price
65	  → setLicPoeMargin          → lic_poe_sell_price
66	  → setLicWhseMargin         → lic_whse_sell_price
67	```
68	
69	All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell.
70	
71	---
72	
73	## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips
74	
75	The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON).
76	
77	The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`.
78	
79	---
80	
81	## AG Grid patterns specific to this module
82	
83	### Async valueSetters
84	
85	Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
86	- Return `true` on success
87	- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
88	- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)
89	
90	### Column identity: never hardcode field names
91	
92	```typescript
93	// Correct
94	params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]
95	
96	// Wrong
97	params.data['CBM/pc']
98	```
99	
100	### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

# Configuration

## Environment files

Angular replaces `src/environments/environment.ts` at build time based on the `--configuration` flag. There are four environments:

| File | Used when | API target |
|---|---|---|
| `environment.ts` | Local dev (`ng serve`) | `http://localhost:5004/api/...` |
| `environment.prod.ts` | `--configuration=production` | `https://api.designflow.app/api/...` |
| `environment.staging.ts` | `--configuration=staging` | `https://api.sandbox.designflow.app/api/...` |
| `environment.sandbox.ts` | `--configuration=sandbox` | `https://api.alsand.designflow.app/api/...` |

Each environment file exposes:

```typescript
export const environment = {
  production: boolean,    // affects Angular's production mode (tree-shaking, etc.)
  url: string,            // /api/core base URL
  tracking: string,       // /api/tracking base URL
  item_master: string,    // /api/item_master base URL
  data_sync: string,      // /api/data_sync base URL
  azureClientId: string,  // Azure AD app registration client ID (same across all envs)
  azureTenantId: string,  // Azure AD tenant ID (same across all envs)
};
```

`azureClientId` and `azureTenantId` are the same value in all four environment files — there is one Azure AD app registration shared across all environments. They are not secrets (they appear in OAuth network requests) and are committed in plain text.

**There are no runtime environment variables injected into the frontend container.** All config is compiled into the Angular bundle. To change an API URL, you must rebuild.

## Build script to configuration mapping

| npm script | Angular config | NODE_ENV (Docker) | Environment file |
|---|---|---|---|
| `build:production` | `production` | `production` | `environment.prod.ts` |
| `build:develop` | `staging` | `develop` | `environment.staging.ts` |
| `build:sandbox-albert` | `sandbox` | `sandbox-albert` | `environment.sandbox.ts` |

The Dockerfile receives `NODE_ENV` as a build argument and calls `yarn build:${NODE_ENV}`. Cloud Build sets `NODE_ENV` via the `_APP_ENV` substitution variable.

## Cloud Build substitution variables

These are set on the Cloud Build trigger (not in any config file). They override the placeholders in `cloudbuild.yaml`:

| Variable | Example value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `_REGION` | `us-central1` | GCP region for Artifact Registry and Cloud Run |
| `_SERVICE_NAME` | `designflow-frontend` | Cloud Run service name |
| `_APP_ENV` | `production` | Passed as `NODE_ENV` to Docker; selects the build script |
| `_APP_NAME` | `designflow-frontend` | Used in the Artifact Registry image path |
| `_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME` | `designflow` | Artifact Registry repository name |
| `_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME` | `sa-...@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com` | GCP service account for Cloud Run |
| `_PORT` | `80` | Port the nginx container listens on |
| `_CPU` | `1` | Cloud Run CPU allocation |
| `_MEMORY` | `512Mi` | Cloud Run memory allocation |
| `_CONCURRENCY` | `80` | Requests per container instance |
| `_MAX_INSTANCES` | `10` | Cloud Run scaling limit |
| `_PLATFORM` | `managed` | Cloud Run platform |

## Angular.json build options

Key flags set per configuration in `angular.json`:

| Config | `optimization` | `sourceMap` | `outputHashing` |
|---|---|---|---|
| default (dev) | false | true | none |
| production | true | false | all |
| staging | false (or true) | true | all |
| sandbox | false | true | all |

## ConfigService (runtime config)

`src/app/helpers/services/config.service.ts` is loaded via Angular's `APP_INITIALIZER` before the app bootstraps. It fetches `src/assets/config.json` — a static file committed to the repo (not injected at runtime). This is separate from the compile-time `environment.ts`.

**Keys in `assets/config.json`:**

| Key | Type | Used by |
|-----|------|---------|
| `DIGITAL_OCEAN_PUBLIC_URL` | string | `image-upload`, `file-upload-dialog`, `upload-file` — constructs public URLs after presigned PUT uploads. **Must match `DO_PUBLIC_URL` on the backend.** Customer logo uploads now get their URL from the backend's `POST /setObjectPublic` response, but this key is still required for item/file uploads. |

# RFQ Pricing Math

**Only read this file when working on RFQ calculations, margin formulas, or sell-price logic.**
Skip it for UI changes, routing, auth, grid wiring, or any non-calculation work.

---

## Business context

The company buys licensed home decor products from factories in China/India (FOB factory terms)
and sells to major US retail chains. The INCOTERM columns in the RFQ grid describe the
**customer-side** delivery terms — i.e., how much logistics cost falls on us vs. the customer.

75% of products are **licensed** (Disney, WB, NBCUniversal, Paramount, etc.) and carry a royalty.
Generic products carry no royalty. This splits every INCOTERM into a Lic and Gen variant,
producing 8 pricing columns total.

---

## The 8 columns

| colId prefix | INCOTERM | Type | What we pay (our cost basis) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fob_` | FOB | — | Customer takes at China/India port; they pay freight + duty |
| `mddp_` | mDDP | — | Customer takes at China/India port; we clear US customs + pay duty |
| `poe_` | POE | — | We pay freight + duty; customer picks up at US port |
| `whse_` | WHSE | — | We pay freight + duty + drayage + warehouse; customer picks up from our LA/NJ warehouse |
| `gen_*` | any | Generic | No royalty |
| `lic_*` | any | Licensed | Royalty applies; FOB royalty rate is higher (stored separately per licensor) |

---

## Inputs

| Field | colId | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Factory FOB price | `fob_cost` | Vendor quote |
| Agent commission | `agent` | % — sourcing agent fee |
| Duty rate | `duty_rate` | % — US customs tariff. **May arrive as `{ title: "8" }` or raw string** — always access as `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0` |
| Duty dollar | `duty_rate_dollar_amount` | Fixed $ amount added on top of the % duty |
| Carton dimensions | `carton_l`, `carton_w`, `carton_h` | cm — from factory |
| Case pack | `casepack` | Pieces per carton |
| Container cost | `container_price` | $ per full container |
| Warehouse cost | `whsecost` | $ per full container equivalent |
| Dilution | `dilution` | % — amount retailer deducts from invoice before paying; royalty still owed on full invoice amount |
| Logistics load | `logistics_load` | % — additional logistics deduction from sell price |
| Royalty (FOB Lic) | `lic_fob_royalty` | % — auto-populated from licensor table `fob_royalty_rate` when License field is set |
| Royalty (mDDP/POE/WHSE Lic) | `lic_mddp_royalty`, `lic_poe_royalty`, `lic_whse_royalty` | % — auto-populated from licensor table `royalty_rate` |
| Royalty (Gen, all terms) | `gen_fob_royalty`, etc. | Always 0 |
| Target margin | `gen_fob_margin`, `lic_fob_margin`, … | % — entered by sourcing team (Scenario 1) |
| Customer sell price | (entered per order) | $ — entered when customer dictates price (Scenario 2) |

---

## Intermediate calculations

These are computed values; code lives in `equation.ts` helpers.

```
cost_incl_agent       = fob_cost × (1 + agent / 100)

cost_incl_duty        = cost_incl_agent × (1 + duty_rate / 100) + duty_dollar

CBM_per_pc            = (carton_L_cm × carton_W_cm × carton_H_cm / 1,000,000) / casepack

freight_per_pc        = (container_cost / 60) × CBM_per_pc
                        ↑ 60 CBM per container (not 56 — confirmed by Albert, matches code)

whse_cost_per_pc      = (warehouse_cost / 60) × CBM_per_pc

LDP_Port              = cost_incl_duty + freight_per_pc
                        (used as cost basis for POE and as input to WHSE)

LDP_Whse              = LDP_Port + whse_cost_per_pc
                        = cost_incl_duty + freight_per_pc + whse_cost_per_pc
```

**Cost basis by INCOTERM:**

| INCOTERM | Cost basis passed to sell-price formula |

## 4a. Branch, sync, test, and PR workflow

All feature work for this sandbox happens on `sandbox-albert`.

Before adding new code:
1. Confirm the local branch is `sandbox-albert`.

600	**Rules added:**
601	
602	**Rule A — commonDefaults is the final fallback, not `undefined`.** Before asserting `toBeUndefined()` on any colDef property, read `commonDefaults` in the config file. The final shape is `{ ...commonDefaults, ...overrides }`. If `commonDefaults` sets the property, removing it from overrides gives you the default value, NOT `undefined`. When removing a property from a colDef, remove it from `commonDefaults` too if it belongs there.
603	
604	**Rule B — Read the full colDef spread before writing a spec.** `addColumn()` produces `{ ...commonDefaults, icons: ..., ...overrides, context: { roles } }`. Never write a spec assertion about a colDef property without verifying that exact merged shape — read the `commonDefaults` block first, then the override.
605	
606	**Rule C — No `require()` in spec files that use ES module imports.** All spec files use `import { readFileSync } from 'fs'` etc. at the top. Never add inline `require('fs')` calls inside the file body — use the already-imported binding.
607	
608	**Rule D — Verify spec assertion values against source before committing.** After any change that removes or renames a colDef/gridOptions property: (1) grep the spec files for the property name, (2) trace the actual runtime value from the source (including defaults/fallbacks), (3) confirm the assertion matches. Do not assume the value will be `undefined` just because an override was deleted.
609	
610	---
611	
612	### 2026 — Add New Item: Licensor dropdown silently did nothing on click (5 commits to fix)
613	
614	**What happened:** Clicking the Licensor `mat-select` in the Add New Item dialog did absolutely nothing — no panel, no error, no console output.
615	
616	**Root cause:** `MatSelect._canOpen()` in Angular Material has this guard:
617	```js
618	_canOpen() {
619	  return !this._panelOpen && !this.disabled && this.options?.length > 0 && !!this._overlayDir;
620	}
621	```
622	The `license` array is empty (`[]`) until the user selects a Division AND `getMerchGroups()` completes its HTTP call. With zero `mat-option` children, `options.length === 0`, so `_canOpen()` returned `false` on every click — completely silently.
623	
624	**Why it was hard to find:** Three plausible-but-wrong hypotheses were chased first:
625	1. `[(value)]="selectedLicense"` conflicting with `[formControl]` (real bug, but not root cause of no-open)
626	2. NG0955 duplicate track keys from `@for (value of priceCode; track value)` on an all-`"X"` array (real bug, but not root cause)
627	3. `mat-select ContentChildren` not picking up options rendered via `| async` in `@for` (real bug at `licenseOptions | async` was replaced with plain `license` array, but still didn't open)
628	
629	**Recovery:** (`51b4d80`) Start `licenseControler` as `disabled: true`. Update `syncLicensorDisabledState()` to also disable when `!selectedDivisionCode || license.length === 0`. Call `syncLicensorDisabledState()` from `getMerchGroups()` (after HTTP populates `license`) and from `clearMerchGroupData()` (when license list is cleared). Licensor now shows greyed-out until a Division is selected and data loads.
630	
631	**Rules added:**
632	
633	**Rule A — If a `mat-select` silently does nothing on click, check `options.length` first.** The Angular Material source guard is `options?.length > 0`. A select with zero `mat-option` children will never open, with no error or warning. Use browser DevTools → Elements to count `mat-option` children. If zero, the data source is the problem, not the component.
634	
635	**Rule B — `mat-select` options must come from a synchronous array, not `| async`.** `MatSelect` discovers options via `@ContentChildren(MatOption)`. Options rendered asynchronously inside `@for` via `| async` can be missed by the query. Use a plain component array (e.g. `license: any[] = []`) and populate it directly, as Division does with `divisionCodeList`. Never use `someObservable | async` as the source for `mat-option` inside `mat-select`.
636	
637	**Rule C — Do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]` on `mat-select`.** `[formControl]` and `[(value)]` are from two different forms APIs and both try to own the value. Using both together produces undefined behavior. Pick one; prefer `[formControl]` for reactive forms.
638	
639	**Rule D — Disable a `mat-select` programmatically when its options are not yet loaded.** If options depend on a prior user action or an async HTTP call, disable the FormControl until the data arrives. `mat-select` with zero options silently refuses to open — the user has no idea why. Greyed-out with a visual state is always better than "looks enabled, does nothing."
640	
641	---
642	
643	### 2026 — AI chat input sent `[object HTMLInputElement]` to the API
644	
645	**What happened:** The AI HTS classifier in the duty rate dialog sent literal `"[object HTMLInputElement]"` as the user's message instead of the actual typed text.
646	
647	**Root cause:** The template had `<input #chatInput ...>` (a template reference variable) and the component had `chatInput: string` (a property). In Angular template expressions, the template ref `#chatInput` shadows the component property `chatInput`. `[(ngModel)]="chatInput"` bound to the HTMLInputElement object, not the string. When `.toString()` was called implicitly, it produced `"[object HTMLInputElement]"`.
648	
649	**Recovery:** (`7ca95c8`, `2cf74cf`) Renamed template ref to `#chatInputRef`. Replaced `[(ngModel)]="chatInput"` with native `[value]="chatInput"` + `(input)="onChatInput($event)"` binding which reads `(event.target as HTMLInputElement).value` — completely immune to template-variable shadowing.
650	
651	**Rule added:** Never give a template reference variable (`#name`) the same name as a component property. When debugging a field that sends wrong values, check whether a `#name` ref exists on or near the element — it will shadow the component property in all template expressions.
652	
653	---
654	
655	### 2026 — License/Div grid cell editor: sometimes didn't open, couldn't close by clicking
656	
657	**What happened:** The multi-select cell editors for License and Div columns in the RFQ grid were unreliable: the panel sometimes didn't open on click, and when it did open, clicking outside to dismiss it didn't work (had to press ESC).
658	
659	**Root cause (can't close):** The cell editor template had `(click)="select.open()"` as a workaround to force the panel open. Angular Material's CDK overlay fires a `backdropClick` event when the user clicks outside the panel, which closes it — but the click also bubbled to the host element, triggering `select.open()` again immediately. The close and re-open happened in the same event loop tick, making it appear the panel couldn't be dismissed.
660	
661	**Root cause (sometimes doesn't open):** `ngAfterViewInit` used a 1 ms `setTimeout` to call `select.open()`. But `ngAfterViewInit` runs before the HTTP call in `ngOnInit` completes. At 1 ms, the options were not yet loaded, the panel opened empty, and Angular Material auto-closed an empty panel. The user saw nothing.
662	
663	**Recovery:** (`0d6fb1c`) Removed `(click)="select.open()"` entirely. Added `_openMultiSelectWhenReady()` (a `setTimeout(0)` guard with `_destroyed` flag) called at the end of the HTTP response handler in `ngOnInit`, after `filteredOptions` is populated. This ensures `open()` is called only after data is loaded and the view is stable.
664	
665	**Rule added:** Never add `(click)="select.open()"` to a `mat-select` host element. It creates a re-open loop that prevents the CDK backdrop from closing the panel. To auto-open a `mat-select` inside a grid cell editor, call `select.open()` programmatically after the data has loaded, using a `setTimeout(0)` to defer past the current change-detection cycle.
666	
667	---
668	
669	### 2026-05-25 — Production SSO: missing static assets
670	
671	**What happened:** Microsoft SSO silently failed on `designflow.app`. The popup appeared briefly, closed, and the app showed "Microsoft sign-in failed."
672	
673	**Root cause:** The production nginx image was built from a commit predating the MSAL static assets. `GET /assets/auth-redirect.html` returned 404. Without this file, the OAuth popup has no bridge script to call `broadcastResponseToMainFrame()`, so the parent window never receives the token.
674	
675	**Recovery:** Applied a Docker patch layer (`gcloud builds submit`) to copy both assets into the existing production image without a full rebuild. Deployed as revision `popcre-frontend-prod-00018-62c`.
676	
677	**Rule:** `src/assets/auth-redirect.html` and `src/assets/msal-redirect-bridge.min.js` are required in every deployed image. They are committed to the repo, so any build from a current commit automatically includes them. Only becomes an issue if a pre-SSO image is manually redeployed.
678	
679	---
680	
681	### 2026-05-26 — SSO 401 across all environments: BFF OIDC audience mismatch
682	
683	**What happened:** `POST /api/core/auth/azure` returned 401 on `alsand.designflow.app` (and would have failed on sandbox and production too). Cloud Run logged: `The request was not authorized to invoke this service. The access token could not be verified.`
684	
685	**Root cause:** The BFF uses `CORE_BACKEND_URL` as both the proxy target and the OIDC token audience when calling backends. `CORE_BACKEND_URL` was set to the custom domain (`https://api.core.alsand.designflow.app`). Cloud Run validates the OIDC token's `aud` claim against the service's `.run.app` URL. The custom domain does not work as an OIDC audience even though it correctly routes traffic via a Cloud Run domain mapping.
686	
687	**Recovery:** Updated `CORE_BACKEND_URL` in all three BFF Cloud Run service configurations to the `.run.app` service URL. Also updated `designflow-bff/env/.env.*` to match. Full gcloud commands recorded in `docs/deployment.md` § 2026-05-26 SSO 401 incident.
688	
689	**Rule:** BFF `*_BACKEND_URL` values must always be `.run.app` service URLs. See `designflow-bff/AGENTS.md` for the permanent explanation.
690	
691	---
692	
693	### 2026-05-27 — RFQ view save broken: saved column order lost after page refresh
694	
695	**What happened:** Drag a column (e.g. `Res`) to a new position → click "Save Current" in the View dropdown → refresh the page → the same view name is selected but the column is back in its original position.
696	
697	**Root cause:** Race between `applyGridViewStateForViewName` (HTTP response) and `onFirstDataRendered` (RAF-deferred). When the HTTP response arrived first:
698	1. `applyColumnState()` applied the correct view layout ✓
699	2. `canPersistGridState` was still `false` → `saveStateToSession()` skipped → sessionStorage was NOT updated
700	3. Later: `onFirstDataRendered` fired → `restoreStateFromSession()` read the stale pre-navigation session snapshot → called `applyColumnState()` again with the old default layout, overwriting the view.
701	
702	**Recovery:** (`6cb2dd82`) Added `_saveViewStateToSession()` — an unconditional session write that bypasses the `canPersistGridState` guard. Called immediately after every `applyColumnState` call inside `applyGridViewStateForViewName` (both the new `GridViewState` path and the legacy `getSelectedGridView` fallback path). Now `restoreStateFromSession` always reads the correct view layout.
703	
704	**Rule added:** Any code path that calls `applyColumnState` to apply a persisted view must also call `_saveViewStateToSession()` immediately after, before any async work. Never rely on `onFirstDataRendered` to leave the session in a consistent state — it fires asynchronously via `requestAnimationFrame` and can arrive after or before any HTTP response.
705	
706	---
707	
708	### 2026-05-27 — RFQ hard refresh (reset to defaults) did nothing when clicking sidenav
709	
710	**What happened:** Clicking "RFQ" in the sidenav while already on the RFQ page had no effect. The grid did not reset to its default state.
711	
712	**Root cause:** Angular's default `onSameUrlNavigation` is `'ignore'` — navigating to the current URL is a no-op. Without component recreation, no grid reset could happen.
713	
714	**Recovery:** (`6cb2dd82`, `17c89da5`) Two changes:
715	1. Added `onSameUrlNavigation: 'reload'` to `RouterModule.forRoot` in `app-routing.module.ts` so same-URL navigation recreates the component.
716	2. Modified `sidenav-item.component.ts` `onClick()` to set `sessionStorage['rfq_hard_reset_pending'] = '1'` when the destination URL equals the current URL. `restoreStateFromSession()` reads and clears this flag — when set, it removes `gridState_rfq` from sessionStorage and returns early, letting the component load fresh from the API.
717	
718	**Rule added:** To trigger a component hard-reset via navigation, both pieces are required: `onSameUrlNavigation: 'reload'` (so the router acts) AND a sessionStorage flag (so `restoreStateFromSession` does not immediately re-apply the stale snapshot). The "Reset View" button approach was rejected in favor of using the sidenav click to keep the toolbar clean.
719	
720	---
721	
722	### 2026-05-27 — Step column cell editor slow on first click (duplicate API call + race condition)
723	
724	**What happened:** Clicking into a Step cell for the first time took several seconds to show the 6 options, even though `loadRFQSteps()` already fetched the step data on component init.
725	
726	**Root cause (slowness):** `loadRFQSteps()` populated `this.stepList` but did NOT write to `window['rfq-autocomplete'].step`. So `rfq-autocomplete.component.ts` always saw the cache empty and made a fresh `getRFQStep()` API call on every first-click.
727	
728	**Root cause (race condition):** The cache guard was `if(!window['rfq-autocomplete'].step)`. When a second editor opened while the first was mid-fetch, it saw the truthy `{}` placeholder set before the fetch completed, went to the `else` branch, and read `undefined` from `window['rfq-autocomplete'].step.options` — resulting in an empty dropdown.
729	
730	**Recovery:** (`6cb2dd82`)
731	1. `loadRFQSteps()` now writes `window['rfq-autocomplete'].step = { options: data.map(...) }` after the fetch. First click is instant.
732	2. Autocomplete guard changed from `if(!window['rfq-autocomplete'].step)` to `if(!window['rfq-autocomplete'].step?.options)` — truthy `{}` is no longer treated as a populated cache.
733	
734	**Rule added:** When pre-populating `window['rfq-autocomplete']` caches, always set the full shape including the `options` key in one assignment, never set an empty object first. Guard checks must test for the presence of `options` specifically, not just the parent object, to be safe against in-flight race conditions.
735	
736	---
737	
738	### 2026-05-27 — Smart-quote inch mark left `x12mm` artifact in cleaned description
739	
740	**What happened:** User reported "Classifying: x12mm Anti-fatigue PVC Kitchen Mat" — the CROSS search ran with `x12mm` as part of the search term and returned zero results.
741	
742	**Root cause:** The raw product description contained a curly/Unicode inch mark `"` (U+201C) in a dimension spec like `18"×12mm`. The dimension pre-pass regex `['"]` matches only ASCII — the Unicode `"` slipped through. Step 7 (miscellaneous char strip) converted the curly `"` to a space, producing `18 x12mm`. Step 7b stripped the bare standalone `18`, leaving `x12mm` as the entire cleaned description.
743	
744	**Fix:** Added Step 0 to `desc-cleaner.ts`: normalize all Unicode smart/curly quote and prime variants to their ASCII equivalents (`"` and `'`) before any dimension regex runs. Also added `\bx\d+(\.\d+)?\s*(unit)?\b` artifact cleanup as a belt-and-suspenders fallback for the `x<number>` prefix pattern.
745	
746	**Rule added:** Always normalize smart quotes to ASCII before regex dimension stripping. The artifact cleanup regex catches leftovers but the root fix is normalization in Step 0.
747	
748	---
749

# DESIGNFLOW FRONTEND — Ground-Truth Facts Report

## 1. PROJECT SUMMARY

**What this app is:**
- Angular 21.1.3 Single-Page Application (SPA) for a Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) platform
- Manages the full product journey: RFQ (vendor quoting and margin calculation), Item Library, Standardized Products, Art Piece management, and Production/Licensing Tracking
- Business-critical outcome: correct margin and landed-cost calculations in the RFQ grid (errors directly affect buy prices and retailer quotes)
- Version: 21.1.5 (per package.json)

**Who uses it:**
- Sourcing, merchandising, licensing, and production teams managing product procurement from factories in China/India to retail chains in the US
- Internal administrative roles (admin, production, sourcing_manager) with restricted features
- Vendor/factory users (upload offers, check quote status)

**Key moving parts:**
- **RFQ module** (src/app/pages/rfq/): Master grid with vendor quotes; calculation engines for landed cost, margin chains, sell-price across 8 INCOTERM scenarios (generic and licensed variants)
- **Item Library** (src/app/pages/itemLibrary/): Product master data management
- **Tracking modules** (prod_tracking, licensing_tracking, sample_tracking): Order tracking, royalty calculations, physical sample movement
- **Admin Editor** (src/app/pages/editor/): AI settings, tariff configuration (Section 301, Trump 2025), factory country mapping
- **Auth** (src/app/pages/auth/): Azure AD SSO + JWT token handling
- Backend: separate REST API service (designflow-bff) at api.designflow.app / api.sandbox.designflow.app / api.alsand.designflow.app

---

## 3. CUSTOM-CODE BOUNDARY

**Custom code (always safe to modify):**
- `src/app/pages/` — all feature modules (RFQ, item library, tracking, editor, auth, etc.)
- `src/app/helpers/` — services, models, interceptors, AG Grid configs
- `src/app/components/` — shared reusable components
- `src/app/custom-layout/` — shell layout
- `src/environments/` — per-environment config
- `src/app/app.module.ts`, `src/app/app-routing.module.ts`, `src/app/app.component.ts`

**Framework/template code (modify only with justification, listed in AGENTS.md §5):**
- `src/@vex/` — Vex UI/layout framework (committed into repo, not an npm package)
- Core modifications inventory in AGENTS.md §5 lists intentional changes to:
  - `src/@vex/layout/sidenav/sidenav-item/sidenav-item.component.ts` (RFQ hard-reset flag)
  - `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/` (profile avatar, impersonation shortcut)

**Vendored Vex demo pages (ignore completely per .claudeignore):**
- `src/app/pages/apps/`
- `src/app/pages/ui/`
- `src/app/pages/documentation/`
- `src/app/pages/pages/`

## 4. BUILD / RUN / TEST / LINT

**Framework versions (from package.json):**
- Angular 21.1.3
- TypeScript 5.9.3
- Node.js LTS (Dockerfile uses `node:lts`)
- Yarn 4.13.0 (packageManager field; **use yarn, not npm**)

**Build scripts (npm / yarn):**

| Command | Environment | API target | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `npm run start` | local dev | localhost:5004 | Runs `ng serve` with `--max-old-space-size=8192`; output on http://localhost:4200 |
| `npm run build:production` | production | api.designflow.app | `NODE_ENV=production`, output hashing enabled |
| `npm run build:develop` | staging | api.sandbox.designflow.app | `NODE_ENV=development`, output hashing enabled |
| `npm run build:sandbox-albert` | sandbox (Albert) | api.alsand.designflow.app | `NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert`, output hashing enabled |
| `npm run test` | — | — | Jest; full suite runs ~12 seconds |
| `npm run test --testPathPatterns="pattern"` | — | — | Single test file (e.g., `"rfq.grid.config"`) |
| `npm run test:watch` | — | — | Jest watch mode |
| `npm run e2e` | — | http://127.0.0.1:4200 | Playwright tests (headless) |
| `npm run e2e:headed` | — | — | Playwright with browser visible |
| `npm run e2e:ui` | — | — | Playwright UI inspector |
| `npm run e2e:report` | — | — | Show HTML test report |
| `npm run lint` | — | — | Angular ESLint via `ng lint` |

**Test infrastructure:**
- **Unit tests:** Jest (jest-preset-angular, 56 `.spec.ts` files across the project)
- **E2E tests:** Playwright (config in `playwright.config.ts`, tests in `e2e/tests/`, Chrome only)
- **Test database:** JSDOM for DOM tests
- **CI/CD:** Docker build includes `yarn test` — tests must pass or the image build fails

**Linting:**
- ESLint via Angular CLI (`ng lint`)
- Config: `tslint.json` (legacy name, actually ESLint rules)

**Memory and performance:**
- Build scripts use `--max-old-space-size=8192` due to heavy TypeScript compilation
- Angular dev build is unoptimized for fast iteration; production build has tree-shaking and minification

---

## 5. DEPLOYMENT

**Pipeline:**
```
git push (sandbox-albert / develop / main)
  → Cloud Build trigger (auto-fired)
    → Step 1: docker build
         - ARG NODE_ENV from _APP_ENV substitution variable
         - ARG COMMIT_SHA for version tracking
         - Dockerfile Stage 1 (Node): yarn install → yarn test → yarn build:${NODE_ENV}
         - Dockerfile Stage 2 (nginx-brotli): copy dist/browser → /usr/share/nginx/html
    → Step 2: docker push → Google Artifact Registry
    → Step 3: gcloud run deploy → Google Cloud Run
```

**Dockerfile summary:**
- **Stage 1 (builder):** `node:lts` base
  - Activates Yarn 4.13.0 via corepack
  - `yarn install --immutable` (frozen lockfile)
  - Injects version info into `src/app/version.ts` (commit hash + timestamp in America/New_York TZ)
  - Runs `yarn test` — **tests must pass or build fails**
  - Builds via `yarn build:${NODE_ENV}` (e.g., `build:production`, `build:sandbox-albert`)
  - Output: `dist/browser/`
- **Stage 2 (runtime):** `macbre/nginx-brotli:1.19.10`
  - Copies custom `nginx.conf` to `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf`
  - Copies built app to `/usr/share/nginx/html/`
  - Exposes port 80
  - Serves SPA with brotli compression

**Cloud Build (cloudbuild.yaml):**

| Step | Command | Purpose | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Build | `docker build -t ${_IMAGE} --build-arg=NODE_ENV=${_APP_ENV} --build-arg=COMMIT_SHA=${COMMIT_SHA}` | Build image with environment-specific build command and version | — |
| Push | `docker push ${_IMAGE}` | Push image to Artifact Registry | waitFor: Build |
| Deploy | `gcloud run deploy ${_SERVICE_NAME} --image=${_IMAGE} --region=${_REGION} --port=${_PORT} --cpu=${_CPU} --memory=${_MEMORY} --concurrency=${_CONCURRENCY} --max-instances=${_MAX_INSTANCES} --allow-unauthenticated --platform=${_PLATFORM} --service-account=${_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME}` | Deploy to Cloud Run | waitFor: Push |

**Image tag format:**
```
{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT_ID}/{ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/{APP_ENV}/{APP_NAME}:{BUILD_ID}-{COMMIT_SHA}
```

Example: `us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/designflow/production/designflow-frontend:abc123-d4e5f6a7`

**Cloud Build substitution variables** (set on the trigger in Google Cloud Console, not in YAML):

| Variable | Example | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `_REGION` | `us-central1` | GCP region for Artifact Registry + Cloud Run |
| `_SERVICE_NAME` | `designflow-frontend` | Cloud Run service name |
| `_APP_ENV` | `production` / `develop` / `sandbox-albert` | Selects build script; passed as NODE_ENV to Docker |
| `_APP_NAME` | `designflow-frontend` | Used in image registry path |
| `_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME` | `designflow` | Artifact Registry repository name |
| `_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME` | `sa-...@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com` | GCP service account for Cloud Run |
| `_PORT` | `80` | Port nginx listens on |
| `_CPU` | `1` | Cloud Run vCPU |
| `_MEMORY` | `512Mi` | Cloud Run memory |
| `_CONCURRENCY` | `80` | Requests per container instance |
| `_MAX_INSTANCES` | `10` | Max container replicas |
| `_PLATFORM` | `managed` | Cloud Run platform (managed = serverless) |

**Deployment targets by branch:**

| Branch | Trigger | Cloud Run Service | API Base | Environment |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sandbox-albert` | sandbox trigger | designflow-frontend-sandbox | api.alsand.designflow.app | Albert's dev/test |
| `develop` | develop trigger | designflow-frontend-staging | api.sandbox.designflow.app | Shared staging |
| `main` | production trigger | designflow-frontend-prod | api.designflow.app | Live production |

**Cloud Run configuration:**
- Platform: managed (serverless)
- Auth: `--allow-unauthenticated` (public, no Cloud IAM)
- Scales to zero when idle
- Config (CPU, memory, concurrency, max-instances) set via Cloud Build substitutions

**Rollback:**
```bash
gcloud run revisions list --service=<SERVICE_NAME> --region=<REGION>
gcloud run deploy <SERVICE_NAME> --revision=<REVISION_ID> --region=<REGION>
```

**No GitHub Actions workflows** — all CI/CD is Google Cloud Build.

**Idiosyncratic decisions** (AGENTS.md §11 — intentional quirks):

| Quirk | Why | How to handle |
|---|---|---|
| Angular project name is "vex" | Framework name baked into angular.json | Prefix is `vex-` for generated components; unchanged |
| `postSortRows` instead of `aggFunc` for group ordering | `aggFunc` breaks margin calculation (silent math errors) | Always use `postSortRows` for custom sort logic; never add `aggFunc` to RFQ columns |
| `getMasterFiledName()` function typo (should be `Field`) | Intentionally preserved to maintain compatibility | Do not rename; use as-is |
| Duty rate arrives as `{ title: string }` OR raw number | Backend inconsistency | Always access as `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0` (optional chaining) |
| `window['itemService']` global refs in rfq.component.ts | Legacy pattern (pre-DI Angular) | Avoid in new code; use Angular DI instead |
| `rfqEquation`, `prodEquation`, `licensingEquation` are separate services | Calculation engines are not shared | Each module has its own injectable service; do not merge |
| AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage) | colId is lowercase underscore; field may have spaces/special chars | Always use `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual field name |
| Duty rate stored twice: equation AND evaluated total | Price-for-sales snapshot requires both | Store both in the same row; formula lives in a computed column, snapshot in metadata |
| `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels cell edit | AG Grid v35 feature | Return `false` on validation error; rejection auto-reverts the cell |
| "Price for Sales" cells use native-title tooltip, not shared customTooltip | Performance — native tooltip is instant, no DOM bloat | Keep the cell renderer pattern; do not migrate to customTooltip |
| Angular 21 defaults `standalone: true` — module-declared components need `standalone: false` | New default breaks old modules | Set `standalone: false` on all components declared in NgModule (most RFQ dialogs, grid editors) |
| Vendor-facing UI strings hardcoded bilingual (中文 - English) | No i18n framework; manual dual-language strings | Maintain both languages in every user-facing string; do not add i18n keys |
| Same factory twice on one RFQ item: allowed for factory, blocked for staff | Business logic quirk | Backend enforces; frontend mirrors the logic in `rfq-copy-guard.ts` |
| Detail-grid default sort targets live price colId, not hardcoded one | Configuration-driven safety | Use `params.columnApi.getColumn('price')` to find the live column, not a string literal |
